name: Space Sciences Laboratory (SSL)
relevance: Organized Research Unit (ORU) founded in 1959. Reporting to the Vice Chancellor for Research, SSL focuses on space physics, planetary science, and building hardware for NASA missions. Affiliated with Physics and Astronomy departments.

name: Space Technologies and Rocketry (STAR)
relevance: UC Berkeley's premier high-power rocketry team, founded in Fall 2015. Competes in challenges like the Spaceport America Cup. Formerly known as CalSTAR. Affiliated with the Aerospace Engineering major.

name: Space Technologies at Cal (STAC)
relevance: Founded around 2015, STAC is a student-run organization focusing on CubeSat development (e.g., QubeSat) and high-altitude ballooning. Supported by Berkeley Engineering.

name: Space Enterprise at Berkeley (SEB)
relevance: Founded in October 2016, SEB aims to be the first collegiate team to send a liquid-fueled rocket past the Kármán line. Focused on supersonic and liquid-propellant systems.

name: Space Exploration Society at Berkeley (SESB)
relevance: Founded around 2012-2013. Acts as the de-facto campus representative for the California Space Grant. Focuses on space advocacy and outreach.

name: Undergraduate Astronomy Society (UAS)
relevance: Active student organization affiliated with the Department of Astronomy. Provides mentorship and academic resources.

name: Radio Astronomy Laboratory (RAL)
relevance: Organized Research Unit (ORU) founded in 1958 within the Astronomy Department. Operates radio telescopes and develops signal processing hardware.

name: Berkeley SETI Research Center (BSRC)
relevance: Research center based in the Department of Astronomy and SSL. Home to the Breakthrough Listen initiative.

name: Theoretical Astrophysics Center (TAC)
relevance: Center within the Department of Astronomy that facilitates interdisciplinary research in theoretical astrophysics.

name: Berkeley Space Center (BSC)
relevance: Joint venture at NASA Ames Research Center founded in 2023 between UC Berkeley and NASA.

name: CASPER
relevance: Collaboration led by UC Berkeley (SSL/Astronomy) developing open-source hardware for radio astronomy.

name: BEST Robotics Lab
relevance: Affiliated with Mechanical Engineering. Researches tensegrity structures for space exploration robots.

name: Berkeley Air & Space Center
relevance: Center within Berkeley Engineering focusing on aviation, space technology, and autonomous systems.

name: HiPeRLab
relevance: Affiliated with Mechanical Engineering. Focuses on high-performance UAVs and autonomous flight.

name: SPACE at Berkeley
relevance: Student-run organization focused on NASA Artemis Student Challenges. Affiliated with Aerospace Engineering.

name: Berkeley Center for Cosmological Physics (BCCP)
relevance: Founded in 2007. Affiliated with the Physics department and LBNL.

name: Geospatial Innovation Facility (GIF)
relevance: Affiliated with Rausser College of Natural Resources. Focuses on geospatial technology and remote sensing.

name: SETI@home
relevance: Launched 1999 at SSL. Ceased new data analysis in 2020. Major historical milestone in space research.

name: AIAA at Berkeley
relevance: Student chapter of the American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics. Active since at least the early 2010s.

name: Plasma and Nonlinear Dynamics Group
relevance: Physics group researching space and astrophysical plasmas, such as the solar wind.

name: Aerospace SAE (ASAE)
relevance: Student design team that participates in the SAE Aero Design competition.

name: Undergraduate Astro Lab
relevance: Department of Astronomy lab in Campbell Hall for undergraduate research and telescope access.

name: Berkeley Ground Station
relevance: Part of SSL, facilitates telemetry and control for space missions.

name: Berkeley Center for Theoretical Physics (BCTP)
relevance: Theoretical research including cosmology and dark energy, affiliated with Physics.

name: Planetary Science Group (SSL)
relevance: Interdisciplinary research group at SSL focusing on solar system exploration.
